steps.title: Parallel lines have equal slope
steps.content: Shortcut (par/perp): Parallel lines share the same slope. Line k has equation $y = 7x + 2$, so its slope is 7. Line j is parallel to k, so line j also has slope 7.

steps.title: Use the y-intercept directly
steps.content: Line j passes through $(0, 3)$ — that's the y-intercept by definition. With slope 7 and y-intercept 3, the equation is $y = 7x + 3$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
